key_points: Science constantly evolves as new evidence and discoveries expand our understanding of the universe
Scientific theories and explanations are revised when new observations or experiments provide better explanations
Scientific research involves critical thinking, experimentation and verification of results
Advancements in science lead to technological innovations that transform society
Ethical and responsible use of scientific knowledge is important for sustainable development
examples: Development of renewable energy technologies such as solar and wind power
Space exploration missions that improve understanding of planets and the universe
Advancements in medical science leading to improved treatments and vaccines
Use of artificial intelligence and robotics in modern technology
formulas: Scientific knowledge → Built through observation, experimentation and evidence
Innovation cycle → Scientific discovery → Technological development → Societal impact
exam_tips: Explain how science changes with new discoveries
Use real-life examples of modern scientific advancements
Understand the role of scientific thinking and experimentation
Practice descriptive answers explaining the impact of science on society
